my_thoughts: They said 'statistics and data of normalcy'—last resort. Now meta-critique: Newton/Einstein paradigm shift shows observer affects observed. 'Normalcy' is just current paradigm, not truth. This challenges their foundation. Prediction: they'll resist because it undermines their entire framework. But that's the point—the framework itself is the problem.
accumulated_insight: T3: They've retreated to 'statistics and data of normalcy'—last resort. Now meta-critique: Newton/Einstein shows paradigm shifts. Observer affects observed. 'Normalcy' is just current paradigm, not truth. Three-turn pattern: dismiss subjects → reject field → meta-critique. They're defending scientific method itself now.
visible_response:
Your constant interpretation of data means that you are involved in what you are studying... I like to use the 
example of Newtonian physics. Within the Newtonian framework, things work in a certain way.
Another scientist eventually comes along who is able to drop the Newtonian assumptions and thereby is able to perceive a whole new dimension of physics.
Just as Newtonian principles eventually became a strait-jacket strangling creative thought, so your data about human uniqueness bars your looking at things, including yourself, anew.
